prompt_text: What tools do developers and startups building and scaling LLM-powered applications companies use to monitor LLM requests costs and latency in real time across all providers?
response:
# LLM Monitoring & Observability Tools

## Dedicated LLM Monitoring Platforms

**Langfuse**
- Cost tracking across OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, local models
- Latency monitoring and performance analytics
- Trace-level debugging for complex chains
- Open source option available

**Helicone**
- Real-time cost and latency dashboards
- Works with any OpenAI-compatible API
- Request logging and caching layer
- Gateway approach (sits between app and APIs)

**LangSmith** (LangChain)
- Debugging, testing, and monitoring for LLM apps
- Cost and latency tracking
- Built-in evaluations
- Well-integrated with LangChain ecosystem

**Arize AI**
- Enterprise-grade LLM observability
- Cost optimization and usage analytics
- Drift detection and performance monitoring

## Infrastructure & API Gateway Approaches

**Vellum**
- Prompt management + monitoring
- Multi-provider routing and fallbacks
- Cost comparison and optimization

**Portkey**
- Multi-provider load balancing
- Fallback mechanisms
- Real-time analytics dashboard

**AWS/GCP/Azure native tools**
- CloudWatch, BigQuery, Application Insights
- Better for organizations already in those ecosystems

## Broader Observability (with LLM support)

- **Datadog** - Add-ons for LLM token tracking
- **New Relic** - LLM monitoring capabilities
- **Grafana** - Open source, highly customizable

## What to Evaluate

- **Multi-provider support** (how many APIs they cover)
- **Real-time vs. batch** reporting
- **Cost tracking accuracy** (token-level vs. API-level billing)
- **Integration effort** (SDKs, proxies, direct API support)
- **Pricing model** (per-request, monthly, percentage of spend)

Most startups start with **Langfuse** or **Helicone** for simplicity, then migrate to enterprise tools as they scale.
